stories.headline: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: Israeli forces kill three Palestinians in Gaza, arrest dozens in West Bank
stories.summary: Attacks come as Palestinian groups meet mediators from Egypt, Turkiye and Qatar in Cairo to discuss fragile 'ceasefire'.

stories.headline: Cement company Lafarge found guilty in Syria terrorism financing case
stories.summary: A French court has found cement company Lafarge guilty of financing armed groups during the Syrian war.

stories.headline: Israel summons Italy's ambassador over comments on 'unacceptable attacks' on Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanon was pulled into the Middle East war when Iran-backed group Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel on 2 March, days after the opening salvo of US-Israeli strikes killed Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei.

stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV says he has 'no fear' of Trump administration
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV said he has no intention to debate with Donald Trump after the US president criticised him over his comments on the Iran war. "I have no fear of the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message of the gospel," the pope told reporters aboard the papal plane.

stories.headline: Israeli forces fire tear gas near Palestinian schoolchildren
stories.summary: Israeli forces fired tear gas towards a group of children trying to walk to school in the occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran has made contact and wants deal ‘very badly’

stories.headline: Dollar Flips to Losses After Trump Says Iran Wants a Deal
stories.summary: The dollar erased gains after President Donald Trump said Iran called Monday morning and wants a deal. The remarks follow Trump’s order over the weekend to block the Strait of Hormuz following the deadlock in US-Iran peace talks that pushed oil prices higher.

stories.headline: Israel needs to stop encouraging settler violence, Palestinian FM says
stories.summary: Palestinian foreign minister told Euronews Israel should protect Palestinians in the West Bank as the occupier, and that instead the government supports settler violence. She also said the Iran war had further marginalised Palestinian aspirations to statehood.

stories.headline: Lambasting Pope Leo, Trump risks alienating conservative Catholics
stories.summary: Veteran church observers say an open war of words between a pope and a U.S. president is unprecedented.

stories.headline: Trump’s threat to blockade Hormuz: Why it’s the latest major escalation
stories.summary: Analysts warn Washington's blockade could harm the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
stories.summary: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.

stories.headline: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing
stories.summary: Six people were killed and ten injured after a man went on a stabbing rampage in an Australian mall.

stories.headline: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.headline: Trump Attacks Pope Leo as Too Liberal and ‘Weak on Crime’
stories.summary: President Trump’s lengthy post on social media showed there were really no boundaries when it comes to people he might target, including the leader of the world’s 1.4 billion Catholics.

stories.headline: 'We cannot ask any country to give up its territory,' Magyar says on Ukraine
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Viktor Orbán had largely centred his electoral campaign on Ukraine and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, blaming Kyiv for the Druzhba pipeline blockade to accusations of meddling in Hungarian elections. Will things change with Magyar in power?

stories.headline: Starmer announces talks to address the Strait of Hormuz crisis
stories.summary: Starmer has announced talks to be held this week and co-hosted with France, aiming at diplomatic end to the war on Iran

stories.headline: What do we know about sea mines in and around the Strait of Hormuz?

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.headline: Two Months to get Oil Back Online is 'Optimistic,' Says Rebecca Babin
stories.summary: Rebecca Babin, senior equity trader and managing director at CIBC Private Wealth, said that the markets are using a two month base case as the predicted time it would take to get oil production back up and running after the end of war with Iran, a timeline she finds 'optimistic.' Babin said that even if US oil exports grows to 5 million barrels a day, it still won't be enough to fill in the supply lost to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.  (So

stories.headline: US Stocks Resume Gains, Close to Erasing Iran War-Driven Losses
stories.summary: US equities rose for the eighth time in nine sessions as investors assessed the US Naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz that took effect Monday and parsed through earnings announcements at home.

stories.headline: Venezuela Set to Award Oil Fields to Chevron to Help Grow Output
stories.summary: Chevron Corp. is set to expand its oil footprint in Venezuela, benefitting from its almost 20-year bet to remain in the country amid political and economic turmoil.

stories.headline: Former Staffers Call on Eric Swalwell to Quit Campaign and Resign from House
stories.summary: In a signed letter, they called on law enforcement officials to “open a full and thorough investigation” of the California representative.

stories.headline: Judge dismisses Trump’s lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein drawing
stories.summary: A federal judge on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by President Donald Trump that claimed that the Wall Street Journal defamed him by publishing a drawing of a naked woman it said Trump contributed to a birthday gift for Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.headline: ‘The perception is Carney is a wartime leader’: why Canada’s PM could secure a majority

stories.headline: Who is Peter Magyar, Hungary’s new leader who trounced Viktor Orban?
stories.summary: Magyar's centre-right Tisza party has won a two-thirds majority in Hungary's parliamentary elections.

stories.headline: Péter Magyar accuses outgoing foreign minister of destroying confidential documents
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister-elect Peter Magyar has accused outgoing Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó of shredding confidential sanctions-related documents at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, citing insider sources during a press conference in Budapest on Monday. The ministry has not yet responded.

stories.headline: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes

stories.headline: Goldman Shares Fall as Bond Trading Miss Outweighs Equity Record
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s surprise drop in bond- and rates-trading revenue cast a shadow over another record quarter from the bank’s equities team, prompting Chief Executive Officer David Solomon to point the finger at analysts for setting the bar so high.

stories.headline: Goldman Sachs Extends Borrowing Run With $6.5 Billion Bond Sale
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is selling $6.5 billion of US investment-grade notes on Monday, extending a borrowing spree that included a record debt offering earlier this year.

stories.headline: Austin Seeks $1 Billion of Munis for Airport Expansion Plan
stories.summary: Austin is set to bring a $1.18 billion airport revenue bond sale to market on Tuesday as growth in one of the nation’s fastest-expanding metro areas strains capacity at Austin-Bergstrom International Airport.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Stocks Extend Gains as Trump Says Iran Wants Deal: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks climbed to session highs while oil prices pared earlier gains after President Donald Trump said Iran still wanted to make a deal following a deadlock in peace talks and a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. shares dropped in an underwhelming start to the earnings season.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files

stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.
